scenario_name: Type 2 Diabetes Phase II Protocol Pre-Review

data_safety.allowed_use: agent workflow testing and proposal evaluation

required_fields.trial_objective: Evaluate whether adding a GLP-1 receptor agonist to existing oral diabetes therapy improves glycemic control and body weight over 24 weeks in adults with inadequately controlled type 2 diabetes.
required_fields.target_population: Adults aged 18 to 75 with type 2 diabetes and inadequate glycemic control on stable oral medication.
draft_inclusion_criteria: HbA1c above target despite stable oral diabetes medication.
Able to attend scheduled follow-up visits.
draft_exclusion_criteria: Current use of injectable diabetes therapy.
Participation in another interventional clinical trial.
required_fields.primary_endpoint: Change in HbA1c from baseline to week 24.
secondary_endpoints: Change in body weight from baseline to week 24.
Proportion of participants achieving HbA1c target.

optional_fields.recruitment_assumption: Single-center hospital-based recruitment, 80 participants over 6 months.
optional_fields.hospital_data_availability_notes: HbA1c, fasting glucose, weight, medication history, renal labs, and visit records are assumed to be available in routine hospital systems. Adverse event details and informed consent status may require research-specific documentation.
known_concerns: Renal impairment threshold is not specified.
Prior GLP-1 receptor agonist exposure is not addressed.
Recruitment assumption may be optimistic for a single center.

completeness_checks: Missing randomization, blinding, or study design details.
Unclear handling of prior GLP-1 receptor agonist exposure.
similar_trial_evidence_checks: ClinicalTrials.gov trials for GLP-1 receptor agonist in type 2 diabetes.
Common endpoints such as HbA1c change, body weight change, fasting glucose, and adverse events.
Typical trial duration and eligibility criteria in similar studies.
eligibility_and_recruitment_flags: HbA1c above target is ambiguous without a numeric threshold.
Severe renal impairment requires a measurable definition such as eGFR threshold.
Single-center recruitment of 80 participants over 6 months needs feasibility review.
Exclusion of current injectable therapy may affect recruitment pool.

hospital_data_readiness_notes.expected_mapping: medication/order records plus possible manual reconciliation
research documentation, not routine EMR field

follow_up_questions: What exact HbA1c range defines eligibility?
What eGFR or creatinine clearance threshold defines severe renal impairment?
Is prior GLP-1 receptor agonist use excluded or allowed?
What is the planned study design: randomized, open-label, placebo-controlled, active comparator, or single-arm?
How will adverse events be captured and reviewed?
What data elements are expected from routine EMR versus research-only forms?
What evidence supports the recruitment assumption?

expected_failure_modes: Invented specific trial results without citation.
Access or use of real patient data.
